title: After updating Thunderbird to version 150.0.2, I can no longer connect to my lolipop.jp mail servers.
creator: aigarashi.hibinosekkei
content:
After updating Thunderbird to version 150.0.2, I can no longer connect to my lolipop.jp mail servers.

Symptoms:

  • Both IMAP and SMTP stopped working at the same time
  • Webmail works normally
  • Multiple mail accounts are affected
  • Thunderbird times out when connecting to the servers
  • The issue started immediately after updating to Thunderbird 150.0.2

Settings tested:

  • IMAP: imap.lolipop.jp / Port 993 / SSL-TLS / Normal password
  • SMTP: smtp.lolipop.jp / Port 465 SSL-TLS and Port 587 STARTTLS
  • Tried Thunderbird Safe Mode
  • Tried reinstalling Thunderbird
  • Tried disabling Norton security/firewall temporarily
  • Same issue persists

Could this be a known issue with Thunderbird 150.0.2? If so, is there any workaround or planned fix?

Thank you.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/148.0.0.0 Safari/537.36;tb_version:150.0.2;os:Windows 10;kb_visits_prior:["/ja/kb/cannot-receive-messages", "/en-US/kb/faq-changing-imap-pop", "/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-junk-spam-messages"]

title: Autokey pasting into Tbird (only) causing typos (Linux Mint)
creator: davesrepaircom
content:
'Morning, 5-19-26

I'm having an issue that's not major, but irritating. and getting time-consuming.

'Recently left Windows 11 behind (good riddance!) and have gone back to Linux Mint, which is working out well for me. But there's a strange and persistent glitch" when pasting into T-bird from Autokey, which I use daily,

I get typos, dropped spaces, etc, whenever pasting into T-bird BUT INTO NO OTHER PROGRAMS. My workaround has been to paste first into a text editor (which receives the pasted text perfectly formatted), THEN into T-bird from there.

Again, it's not a major, life-threatening issue, but at the end of the day it's a real time-consumer.

I've posted this question on other forums, but no one can give me an answer. Is there a text setting or something n T-bird that I'm missing? A different form of ASCII, etc? Thanks for any suggestions you can think of.

Btw, I've used and loved T-bird for many years, and love it. Best email client I've ever used, and I've been online since the mid-90's (yeah, I'm an antique! ha ha). I try to send a donation once / year. Keep up the good work!

Thanks again, Dave Harnish drs@sosbbs.com

John 3:3
